name: Startup Runway Calculator
description: Show months of runway before funds run out.

example.explanation: Runway estimates how many months your startup can operate at current burn.

faqs.question: How long can a business operate before break-even?
faqs.answer: Startup: 12-24 months typical. Depends on CAC payback and unit economics.

faqs.question: What's a good CAC payback period?
faqs.answer: < 12 months ideal. 12-18 months acceptable. > 18 months needs unit economics review.

faqs.question: Should I focus on revenue or profit?
faqs.answer: Profit. Growing revenue at lower margins is unsustainable. Aim for healthy margins first.

faqs.question: How do I optimize pricing?
faqs.answer: Value-based pricing > cost-plus > competitor pricing. Test price elasticity; most SaaS can charge 10-20% more.

faqs.question: What's runway and why does it matter?
faqs.answer: Months of operations your cash supports. Always maintain 12+ months runway for stability and negotiating power.

concept: Business calculators help entrepreneurs and freelancers analyze profitability, runway, pricing, and financial metrics for their ventures.
guideSteps: Enter your business financials (revenue, costs, expenses).
Input your pricing strategy or unit economics.
Specify your business parameters (team size, growth rate, etc.).
Click Calculate to see key metrics like break-even, runway, margin.
Use insights to refine pricing, cost management, and growth strategy.
standards: Healthy margin: 30-50% for SaaS, 15-30% for e-commerce, 20-40% for services.
Break-even point: When total revenue = total costs.
Cash runway: Months of operations your current burn rate supports.
CAC payback: Months to recover customer acquisition cost.
